programs.name: New Hampshire Medicaid (Nursing Home Medicaid for seniors)
programs.what_it_does: Covers costs for 24-hour skilled nursing care in nursing homes, including room and board, nursing care, medical supplies, rehabilitation, personal care, and medication management. Participants contribute most of their income toward care costs, retaining a small personal needs allowance.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals who are NH residents, US citizens or qualified immigrants, meet nursing home level of care, with income ≤$2,982/month (2026) and assets ≤$2,500 (single).[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of nursing home costs after income contribution; average NH nursing home cost ~$10,000+/month offset for eligible.[1][3][5]

programs.name: Choices for Independence (CFI) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Provides home and community-based services (HCBS) to allow seniors to receive care at home or in community settings instead of nursing facilities. Services include personal care, home health aides, adult day health, home-delivered meals, environmental modifications, respite care, and medical transportation.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ or disabled adults requiring nursing home level of care, NH residents, income ≤$2,982/month, assets ≤$2,500.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by need; covers personal care hours, modifications up to limits, preventing institutionalization (avg. value equivalent to nursing home costs ~$100K+/year).[1][3]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including primary care, hospitalization, prescription drugs, and supportive services. Coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid benefits through adult day health centers.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified for nursing home level of care, able to live safely in community, dually eligible for Medicare/Medicaid.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage of all needed services; no copays for participants.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Program (MSP) - QMB, SLMB, QI
programs.what_it_does: Helps low-income Medicare beneficiaries pay Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments via Medicaid. Includes QMB (full coverage), SLMB (Part B premium), and QI/SLMB135 (Part B premium for higher incomes); auto-enrolls in Extra Help for drugs.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees 65+ with low income/assets: QMB ≤100% FPL, SLMB 100-120% FPL, QI 120-135% FPL; NH residents.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Part B premium ($174.70/mo 2024) + deductibles/cost-sharing; QI fully federal-funded (~$2,000+/year savings).[2]

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly EBT card benefits to buy healthy food at authorized retailers. Promotes nutrition security for low-income households.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (higher asset disregard), NH residents; income <130% FPL (net).
programs.benefit_value: Avg. $200-300/month per eligible senior household (2026 max ~$291/single).

programs.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help pay heating costs, crisis fuel assistance, and weatherproofing repairs. Prevents shutoffs and supports year-round energy needs.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income NH households including seniors; income ≤60% state median or 150% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: Heating grants $200-1,800; crisis up to $1,000+ per season.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements like insulation, air sealing, and furnace repairs to reduce energy costs. Improves health/safety via reduced drafts and better heating.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income NH households <200% FPL, priority to seniors/disabled/vulnerable.
programs.benefit_value: Free upgrades worth $5,000-15,000/home; lifetime energy savings $2,000+.

programs.name: New Hampshire State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, objective counseling on Medicare options, supplements, drug plans, and appeals. Helps seniors choose/save on coverage and understand rights.[2]
programs.who_its_for: All NH Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, family.
programs.benefit_value: Free 1-on-1 help; avg. savings $1,000+/year via optimal plan selection.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (Council on Aging programs)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious hot meals to homebound seniors 5+ days/week, plus safety wellness checks. Provides frozen weekend meals and nutrition education.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, low-income prioritized.

programs.name: New Hampshire Caregiver Support System
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite grants, caregiver training, support groups, and counseling to relieve family caregivers. Funds short-term relief services like in-home care or adult day.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+ or disabled adults; income-based.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,600/year; training free.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid community service work training leading to unsubsidized employment. Pays minimum wage for 20 hrs/week at nonprofits.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ (≤125% FPL), NH residents.

programs.name: New Hampshire Legal Assistance (NHLA) Senior Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for income/asset issues, benefits, housing, consumer problems, and elder abuse. Includes representation, advice, and community education.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+, NH residents (priority to most vulnerable).
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services worth $200+/hr; resolves benefits claims/back payments.

programs.name: New Hampshire Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care facilities. Investigates complaints, resolves issues, and promotes quality of life/rights.[3]
programs.who_its_for: All residents/patients in NH long-term care facilities, families.
programs.benefit_value: Free confidential advocacy; resolves ~85% complaints without litigation.
